description: International standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS). Annex A controls aligned to ISO/IEC 27001:2022.

controls.title: Information Security Policies
controls.description: Define, approve, publish, and communicate an information security policy and topic-specific policies aligned to business strategy and regulatory requirements.
controls.guidance: Policies must be approved by top management, reviewed at planned intervals or after significant changes, and communicated to all relevant personnel and external parties.

controls.title: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: Assign and communicate information security responsibilities across the organization.
controls.guidance: Document all information security roles. Ensure accountability is established for the protection of assets. Include responsibilities in job descriptions and contracts.

controls.title: Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Separate conflicting duties and areas of responsibility to reduce opportunities for unauthorized access, fraud, or misuse.
controls.guidance: Identify conflicting roles (e.g., developer and production deployer). Implement compensating controls where segregation is impractical. Document exceptions and their mitigations.

controls.title: Threat Intelligence
controls.description: Collect and analyze information about information security threats to produce actionable threat intelligence.
controls.guidance: Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds. Monitor CVE databases and vendor security advisories. Integrate threat intelligence into risk assessment and incident response processes.

controls.title: Information Security in Project Management
controls.description: Integrate information security into project management throughout the project lifecycle.
controls.guidance: Include security requirements in project initiation. Conduct security reviews at key project milestones. Ensure security sign-off before project go-live.

controls.title: Supplier Relationships Security
controls.description: Define and implement processes to manage information security risks associated with using products or services from suppliers.
controls.guidance: Conduct supplier security assessments before engagement. Include security requirements in supplier contracts. Monitor supplier compliance periodically. Manage risks from the supplier's supply chain.

controls.title: Pre-Employment Screening
controls.description: Verify the identity and background of all candidates for employment commensurate with the sensitivity of information they will access.
controls.guidance: Background checks should include identity verification, employment history, criminal record check (where legally permitted), reference checks, and qualification verification.

controls.title: Information Security Awareness, Education and Training
controls.description: Ensure all personnel receive appropriate information security awareness training relevant to their role, at hire and on a regular basis.
controls.guidance: Provide annual security awareness training for all staff. Deliver role-specific training for technical staff, administrators, and developers. Track completion and conduct phishing simulations.

controls.title: Termination and Change of Employment
controls.description: Protect the organization's information security interests upon termination or change of employment.
controls.guidance: Revoke all access on the last day of employment. Retrieve organization assets. Require departing employees to return data. Brief employees on confidentiality obligations that survive termination.

controls.title: Remote Working Security
controls.description: Implement controls to protect information accessed, processed, or stored at remote working sites.
controls.guidance: Enforce VPN use for accessing internal systems remotely. Apply device management policies to remote devices. Provide guidance on securing home office environments. Conduct periodic reviews of remote access.

controls.title: Physical Security Perimeters
controls.description: Define and use physical security perimeters to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.
controls.guidance: Implement physical barriers (locked doors, badge access). Use CCTV surveillance. Maintain visitor logs. Conduct physical security audits at least annually.

controls.title: Physical Entry Controls
controls.description: Secure areas must be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure only authorized personnel are allowed access.
controls.guidance: Use multi-factor physical access (badge + PIN) for sensitive areas. Escort visitors at all times. Review access logs regularly. Remove access for leavers immediately.

controls.title: Equipment Security
controls.description: Protect equipment from physical and environmental threats and hazards to reduce the risk of unauthorized access and loss or damage.
controls.guidance: Secure server equipment in locked racks. Protect against environmental threats (fire, flood, power surge). Implement a clear desk and clear screen policy. Secure equipment before disposal.

controls.title: Secure Disposal or Re-Use of Equipment
controls.description: Ensure all sensitive data and licensed software are securely removed from equipment prior to disposal or re-use.
controls.guidance: Use certified data destruction methods (NIST SP 800-88). Maintain records of disposal. Obtain certificates of destruction from third-party disposal vendors.

controls.title: User Endpoint Devices
controls.description: Protect information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices.
controls.guidance: Apply mobile device management (MDM). Enforce full disk encryption on all endpoints. Enable remote wipe capability. Keep operating systems and applications patched.

controls.title: Privileged Access Rights
controls.description: Restrict and carefully manage the allocation and use of privileged access rights.
controls.guidance: Use privileged access workstations (PAWs) or just-in-time access. Log all privileged sessions. Review privileged accounts quarterly. Enforce MFA for all administrative access.

controls.title: User Authentication
controls.description: Ensure the identity of users and systems is verified as a prerequisite for granting access to information systems.
controls.guidance: Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) on all externally-facing systems and sensitive internal systems. Use strong password policies (minimum 12 characters, complexity). Disable accounts after prolonged inactivity.

controls.title: Cryptography and Key Management
controls.description: Implement and manage the use of cryptography to protect the confidentiality, authenticity, and integrity of information.
controls.guidance: Use TLS 1.2+ for data in transit. Use AES-256 for data at rest. Maintain a key management procedure covering key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction. Review cryptographic algorithms annually.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Identify and remediate technical vulnerabilities in a timely manner to reduce the risk of exploitation.
controls.guidance: Run automated vulnerability scans weekly. Patch critical vulnerabilities within 30 days, high within 60 days. Conduct penetration tests annually. Maintain a remediation tracking process.

controls.title: Network Security
controls.description: Protect networks and network services from unauthorized access, use, and attack.
controls.guidance: Segment networks based on sensitivity. Use firewalls and intrusion detection/prevention systems. Monitor network traffic. Restrict and log remote access connections.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Produce, store, protect, and analyze event logs to detect, investigate, and recover from anomalous or malicious events.
controls.guidance: Enable logging on all critical systems. Centralize logs in a SIEM. Retain logs for at least 12 months. Configure alerts for suspicious activity. Review logs daily for critical systems.

controls.title: Information Backup
controls.description: Maintain and regularly test copies of information, software, and systems to ensure recovery from data loss incidents.
controls.guidance: Follow the 3-2-1 backup rule (3 copies, 2 media types, 1 offsite). Encrypt backups at rest. Test restoration monthly. Document recovery time and recovery point objectives (RTO/RPO).

controls.title: Secure Development Lifecycle
controls.description: Establish and apply rules for the secure development of software and systems.
controls.guidance: Follow secure coding standards (OWASP, SANS). Conduct static analysis (SAST) in CI/CD pipelines. Require security code reviews for all changes. Use dependency scanning to detect vulnerable libraries.

controls.title: Data Leakage Prevention
controls.description: Apply measures to detect and prevent the unauthorized disclosure or exfiltration of sensitive information.
controls.guidance: Deploy DLP tools on email, endpoints, and cloud storage. Classify data and apply sensitivity labels. Monitor and alert on bulk data transfers. Restrict use of removable media.
